articles.title: How to start a course

steps.title: Go to My Courses from the left menu
steps.description: Open your course library to see everything you are enrolled in and what is ready to begin.

steps.title: Choose a course card that interests you
steps.description: Each course card shows the topic, progress snapshot, and the quickest path back into learning.

steps.title: Click the Start Learning button
steps.description: This opens the course detail page and takes you into the lesson flow for that subject.

steps.title: Watch lessons and complete activities
steps.description: Finish videos, notes, assignments, and quizzes to keep your progress bar moving forward.

articles.tip: Complete lessons regularly to unlock badges, improve your streak, and climb the leaderboard.

articles.title: Understand your course progress

steps.title: Look for completion labels on your course cards
steps.description: Cards can show not started, in progress, or completed states so you know where to jump back in.

steps.title: Open the course detail page for deeper tracking
steps.description: Inside the course, lesson progress and quiz completion give you a more detailed status view.

steps.title: Finish pending activities to update the tracker
steps.description: Progress updates after required lessons and activities have been completed successfully.

articles.tip: If progress looks stuck, refresh the page after finishing the latest required activity.

articles.title: Use the Learning Journey page
articles.summary: See where you are headed next

steps.title: Open Learning Journey from the sidebar
steps.description: This page gives you a visual route through your current path and upcoming milestones.

steps.title: Review unlocked and locked milestones
steps.description: Unlocked items are ready now, while locked ones usually depend on finishing earlier courses.

steps.title: Use milestone tooltips for timing and rewards
steps.description: Milestones can show bonus windows, EduCoin rewards, and helpful completion hints.

articles.tip: Check this page before starting a new course so you focus on the next highest-impact milestone.

articles.title: Submit assignments
articles.summary: Turn in your homework on time

steps.title: Open your course or classroom assignment area
steps.description: Assignments usually appear inside the relevant course detail page or classroom section.

steps.title: Attach the required file or response
steps.description: Double-check the format, title, and instructions before uploading your final work.

steps.title: Press submit and wait for confirmation
steps.description: A successful submission should show a confirmation state or message in the interface.

articles.tip: Upload a few minutes early whenever possible so you have time to fix any file issues.

articles.title: Take a quiz

steps.title: Open the quiz from your lesson or classroom
steps.description: Quiz access usually appears after lesson content or in the classroom activity list.

steps.title: Read every question carefully before answering
steps.description: Take your time and use lesson notes or memory prompts when allowed.

steps.title: Submit and review your score
steps.description: After submission, check the result and any teacher feedback that appears.

articles.tip: If retries are allowed, review weak areas first instead of immediately reattempting.

articles.title: Upload late work the right way

steps.title: Check whether the assignment still accepts uploads
steps.description: Some tasks allow late submissions while others may require teacher approval first.

steps.title: Add a short note if your teacher expects context
steps.description: A simple explanation helps the reviewer understand why the assignment arrived late.

steps.title: Confirm the upload status after sending
steps.description: Make sure the new file appears in the assignment area rather than staying in draft form.

articles.tip: When in doubt, message the teacher after uploading so they know to review the new submission.

articles.title: Earn badges

steps.title: Complete required lessons and activities
steps.description: Badges are usually connected to consistent completion, performance, or milestone events.

steps.title: Watch for badge updates in progress areas
steps.description: Your profile, learning journey, or reward-related pages may show newly unlocked badges.

steps.title: Keep a steady streak to unlock more opportunities
steps.description: Regular activity often matters just as much as single high scores.

articles.tip: Small, steady wins often unlock more rewards than one-off bursts of activity.

articles.title: Find your certificates
articles.summary: Access your earned proof of achievement

steps.title: Open your progress or certificate area
steps.description: Certificates normally appear after the required course or milestone has been fully completed.

steps.title: Choose the certificate you want to view
steps.description: Some learners can unlock multiple recognitions over time, so look for the correct one.

steps.title: Preview before downloading or sharing
steps.description: Check your name, course title, and completion details before using the certificate externally.

articles.tip: If a certificate is missing, confirm the final required activity has been marked complete first.

articles.title: Improve your streak

steps.title: Log in regularly and complete one meaningful task
steps.description: A short, focused session is often enough to keep your progress rhythm alive.

steps.title: Use reminders for your preferred study time
steps.description: Streaks are easier to keep when your learning time fits your actual routine.

steps.title: Check progress visuals for encouragement
steps.description: Seeing visible momentum helps you maintain healthy consistency week to week.

articles.tip: Aim for repeatable habits, not perfect study sessions.

articles.title: Use Educy for revision
articles.summary: Turn quick questions into clearer understanding

steps.title: Ask one focused question at a time
steps.description: Specific prompts usually produce the most useful and understandable answers.

steps.title: Request simpler explanations when needed
steps.description: Educy can often reframe a concept in a clearer or more student-friendly way.

steps.title: Use follow-up prompts to deepen understanding
steps.description: A good conversation usually comes from refining the same topic step by step.

articles.tip: Treat Educy like a revision partner, not a one-shot answer box.

articles.title: Ask Educy for examples
articles.summary: See difficult concepts in a more practical way

steps.title: Name the topic you are struggling with
steps.description: The clearer the topic, the easier it is for Educy to provide useful examples.

steps.title: Ask for a real-world or simple classroom example
steps.description: Concrete examples often make abstract concepts easier to remember.

steps.title: Compare two examples if you still feel unsure
steps.description: Seeing variation can help you understand what changes and what stays constant.

articles.tip: If the first example feels too advanced, ask for one aimed at your grade level.

articles.title: Join a classroom session
articles.summary: Enter class smoothly and on time

steps.title: Open the classroom area from the sidebar
steps.description: This is usually where live sessions, notices, and class-specific activity appear.

steps.title: Select the active or upcoming session
steps.description: Look for timing details and join controls before the session begins.

steps.title: Check audio, instructions, and attendance prompts
steps.description: A quick check at the start helps you avoid missing the first few minutes.

articles.tip: Join a little early if the class includes live discussion or attendance tracking.

articles.title: Read classroom notices

steps.title: Open your classroom feed or notice section
steps.description: Important reminders, due dates, and changes often appear here first.

steps.title: Look for the latest pinned or highlighted message
steps.description: Teachers may pin the most urgent notice to make it easier to spot.

steps.title: Take action on any linked task or reminder
steps.description: Some notices include direct links to lessons, assignments, or forms.

articles.tip: Check notices before asking for help since the answer is often already there.

articles.title: Update notification preferences

steps.title: Open your account settings
steps.description: Notification preferences usually live beside profile and account controls.

steps.title: Choose which alerts matter most
steps.description: Keep important reminders for classes, assignments, and achievements switched on.

steps.title: Save and test the new preference
steps.description: Make sure you still receive the updates that help you stay on track.

articles.tip: Too many alerts can make you ignore important ones, so keep only the essentials enabled.

articles.title: AI-powered study briefs are now live
articles.summary: Get instant recaps of lesson highlights and key updates the moment they are posted.

steps.title: Open a supported course or update stream
steps.description: Recent releases can now show AI-generated summaries at the top of the content area.

steps.title: Scan the short brief before reading in full
steps.description: The summary highlights the most important changes so you can decide where to focus next.

steps.title: Use the brief to jump into lessons faster
steps.description: This is especially useful when a course has new notes, edits, or announcements you missed.

articles.tip: Use the brief as a quick orientation tool, then open the full lesson for the complete context.

articles.title: A faster way to analyze course updates
articles.summary: Explore new notes, document highlights, and grouped updates faster with a clearer reading flow.

steps.title: Open a lesson with attached notes or documents
steps.description: The newest reading experience groups important items more clearly for faster scanning.

steps.title: Use highlighted sections to spot key changes
steps.description: New visual emphasis helps you understand what is fresh without rereading everything.

steps.title: Return to the grouped view when reviewing later
steps.description: This makes it easier to compare new updates against previous material.

articles.tip: If you revisit lessons often, start with highlighted sections before reading the full document again.

articles.title: Stronger reward visibility across your journey
articles.summary: Badges, milestones, and celebration moments are easier to spot throughout the student experience.

steps.title: Check the Learning Journey after completing a lesson
steps.description: Reward moments are now surfaced more clearly after important progress events.

steps.title: Look for new celebration feedback on achievement screens
steps.description: The updated visual treatment helps milestones feel more noticeable and rewarding.

steps.title: Use reward views to understand what to unlock next
steps.description: The refreshed flow makes the next target easier to understand at a glance.

articles.tip: When a badge appears new, open the related milestone and review what unlocked it so you can repeat the pattern.
